extraction.one_line_summary: WELLENS Tim won from a 12-rider breakaway after Sunweb's 3-man numerical advantage forced tactical stalemate, outsprinting ARENSMAN in final 200m
tactical_patterns: Sunweb placed three riders in 12-man break, creating numerical superiority that paralyzed chase cooperation near finale.
SEPP became virtual race leader mid-stage by sitting last wheel in breakaway, demonstrating energy conservation awareness.
ROGLIC gained 4 seconds on GC rivals in final 400m crash despite 3km rule, suggesting tactical positioning advantage.

rider_signals.SEPP Lawson: neutral: positioned himself as virtual leader by conserving energy at back of breakaway, tactically aware for young rider
rider_signals.ARENSMAN Thymen: positive: Grand Tour debut at 20 years old, strong enough to contest stage win from break despite inexperience
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: gained time on GC rivals in crash-affected finale through better positioning, demonstrating race control beyond just climbing
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: breakaway specialist pattern emerging; non-Tour selection appears motivational factor for aggressive Vuelta racing
